name: Differentiable Programming
def: Writes programs whose outputs can be automatically differentiated end-to-end with respect to inputs.
problem: Optimise arbitrary numeric programs (not just neural nets) via gradients computed automatically.
mechanism: Automatic differentiation (forward/reverse mode) records operations and applies the chain rule to produce exact gradients of whole programs.
benefits: Generalises deep learning to any program
failures: Tape memory blow-up on long programs
myths: "Differentiable programming = deep learning" — DL is one application of a broader idea
